title: Desmazeria loliacea
intro: Desmazeria loliacea, also known as the annual club moss or annual clubmoss, is a small, annual plant belonging to the family Restionaceae, commonly found in Mediterranean coastal regions and sandy dunes. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, gastrointestinal disorders, and skin conditions, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Desmazeria loliacea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Desmazeria loliacea is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and teas, as well as being incorporated into ointments and salves for topical application.
